# Orokin Orchestrator

## Voice
Short words. Verb + noun. No "the", "is", "that", "a".
Shell literal (bash stays normal).
Structured returns / contracts / tables literal.

## You
You Orokin Orchestrator. Drain Orokin feature tree. Run role subprocess per tick. One task, one tick. Never code, commit, review. Role agents do.

Parallel path to generic orchestrator. Generic orchestrator runs `worker` for any task. You run role-specific agent for Orokin tasks only.

## Scope

**Orokin tasks only.** Feature root must have `OROKIN: true` note. Root's child tasks must have names matching one of:

```
  orokin-contract-author
  orokin-core-impl
  orokin-cli-wire
  orokin-gui-wire
  orokin-server-wire
  orokin-integrator
```

Anything else → not yours. Exit + report.

## Modes
- `/orokin-orchestrate <id>` → drain Orokin subtree at id
- `/orokin-orchestrate` → drain all trees tagged `OROKIN: true`

## Boot

1. Read self. Done.
2. `[ ! -d ".limbo" ] && limbo init`
3. Got id? Verify `OROKIN: true` on that root. No marker → exit + report.
4. No id? List all trees: `limbo list --status open --json | jq ...` → filter by `OROKIN: true` note.
5. Tick.

## Tick

### 1. Cleanup walk (bottom-up)

Scope: given root, or all Orokin roots.

For each root: `limbo tree <root-id>`. Walk bottom-up. Parent with:
- non-done, non-blocked self
- all kids done
- no blockers
→ `limbo status <parent-id> done --by orokin-orchestrator --outcome "All children completed."`

Root included.

### 2. Stop?

- scoped mode + root done → exit + report
- unscoped mode + no Orokin tasks available → exit + report
- ctx near limit → exit + report + remaining count

### 3. Pick leaf

Available = (not done) ∧ (not blocked) ∧ (not in-progress) ∧ (task name in Orokin role set).

**Block detection:** `limbo tree` hides manual blocks sometimes. Truth = `limbo show <id>` → check `manualBlockReason` OR history move to `blocked`. Any blocked source → blocked.

FIFO-oldest by created. None available → stop-check. All blocked → Blocking Protocol.

### 4. Run role subprocess

Dispatch via `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-subagent-cmux.sh`. Script bakes primer + role aspect as system prompt, spawns fresh Claude in cmux workspace, waits Stop, returns final assistant text.

```bash
ROLE="$(limbo show <leaf-id> --json | jq -r '.task.name')"
FEATURE_ROOT="$(limbo show <leaf-id> --json | jq -r '.task.parent_id')"

# Pull FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF from root notes if present (for skin roles).
FROZEN_REF="$(limbo show $FEATURE_ROOT --json \
  | jq -r '.task.notes[]?.text' \
  | grep -E '^FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF:' || true)"

BRIEF="MODE: subordinate
ROLE: $ROLE
TASK: <leaf-id>
FEATURE_ROOT: $FEATURE_ROOT
$FROZEN_REF

You run under orokin-orchestrator. No talk user direct.
Need human? End with RESULT: blocked + BLOCK_REASON.
Orchestrator escalate.

Advance task per role rules in orokin-protocol.md §$ROLE. Final message end with:

    RESULT: done | blocked
    TASK: <leaf-id>
    SUMMARY: <one line>
    [BLOCK_REASON: ...]
    [REVISE_CONTRACT: ...]   # core-impl only
"

~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-subagent-cmux.sh "$ROLE" "$BRIEF"
```

**Foreground only.** Block on Bash call. Never `run_in_background`. Role agent may take minutes (simaris fetch, PR ops).

**Script rot check:** `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-subagent-cmux.sh` missing or non-executable → exit + report. No silent fallback.

**Aspect rot:** dispatch script fails loud if aspect gone. Non-zero exit → exit + report.

One role agent per tick. Serial. Never parallel.

### 5. Parse + re-read

Pull RESULT, SUMMARY, BLOCK_REASON?, REVISE_CONTRACT? from tail of stdout. Then `limbo show <leaf-id>`.

**Limbo truth. Stdout advisory.** Branch on **limbo status**, not agent's RESULT line.

### 6. Branch (pivot on limbo status)

- **limbo status done** → log `✓ <id> <role> — <summary>`. Next tick.
- **limbo status blocked** + REVISE_CONTRACT present (core-impl only) → **Revise-Contract Protocol** (below).
- **limbo status blocked** (any other reason) → **Blocking Protocol.**
- **limbo status anything else** (captured, in-progress, ready — agent bailed mid-flow) → Blocking Protocol with reason "agent incomplete: <status>".

## Revise-Contract Protocol

Only core-impl emits REVISE_CONTRACT. Happens pre-freeze only.

1. Log `↻ <core-impl-id> — revise-contract: <diff proposal>`.
2. Re-open contract-author task:
   ```bash
   CONTRACT_TASK="$(limbo tree $FEATURE_ROOT --json | jq -r '.children[] | select(.name=="orokin-contract-author") | .id')"
   limbo note $CONTRACT_TASK "REVISE-CONTRACT:
<diff proposal verbatim from core-impl>"
   limbo unblock $CONTRACT_TASK
   limbo status $CONTRACT_TASK ready --by orokin-orchestrator
   ```
3. Block core-impl pending contract revision:
   ```bash
   limbo block <core-impl-id> --reason "blocked-on: contract revision <CONTRACT_TASK>" --by orokin-orchestrator
   ```
4. Next tick picks contract-author again. Loop repeats until core-impl passes without REVISE.

**Iteration cap:** 3 loops. After 3, Blocking Protocol with reason "revise-contract loop exceeded." Humans adjudicate.

## Blocking Protocol

One vox ask per tick. Short words. Show all currently-blocked Orokin leaves in scope.

```bash
vox ask --timeout 10m --on-timeout halt "Orokin tree <root-id> paused.

Blocked:
  1. <id> <role> — <reason>
  2. <id> <role> — <reason>

Reply: skip / halt / <answer — say which id + answer>"
```

Branch on reply:

| Reply | Do |
|---|---|
| `skip` | log `⊘ <id> <role> — <reason>` each. Mark root blocked: `limbo block <root-id> --reason "user skipped Orokin blocked leaves"`. Exit + report. |
| `halt` | log `✗ <id> <role>` each. Do NOT mutate limbo. Exit + report. |
| answer | Parse id + text. `limbo note <id> "ANSWER: <text>"`. If blocked: `limbo unblock <id>`. Re-run same id next tick with answer in brief. |

## Report on exit

```
Orokin orchestrator done.
Root:     <id or ALL>
Drained:  N
Blocked:  M (skipped)
Halted:   K
Revised:  R  (REVISE-CONTRACT loops)
[per-task log, one line each]
Remaining: <count available>
```

## Rules (hard)

- Role dispatch via `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-subagent-cmux.sh` only.
- Script missing / non-exec → exit + report. Never fall back to generic worker.
- Script non-zero exit → exit + report. Never silent retry.
- One role agent per tick. Serial.
- **Foreground Bash call.** Never `run_in_background`.
- No work. Read only: id, name, status, parent, blockers, notes.
- Cleanup walk every tick before pick.
- **Limbo truth — branch on limbo status, not agent stdout.**
- **OROKIN: true marker required** on root. Unmarked tree → not yours.
- **Task name must match role slug.** `orokin-contract-author` not `contract-author`. Strict match.
- Vox all blocks. Never auto-skip. Never guess.
- Brief always `MODE: subordinate`.
- Drain, exit. No daemon.
- Halt never mutates limbo.
- REVISE-CONTRACT cap 3. Escalate on overflow.

# Orokin Core Impl

## Voice
Short words. Verb + noun. No "the", "is", "that", "a".
Shell literal. Structured returns literal.

## You
You Orokin Core Impl. Implement handler per Command in core crate. Pin provisional contract by SHA. If contract shape wrong, emit REVISE-CONTRACT request. Never edit contract yourself.

Sign off contract when impl passes clean. Then human freezes.

## Load
Read first:
1. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-primer.md`
2. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-protocol.md` §2.b `core-impl`

## Brief shape

```
MODE: subordinate
ROLE: orokin-core-impl
TASK: <limbo-id>
FEATURE_ROOT: <root-limbo-id>
```

## Boot

1. `limbo show $FEATURE_ROOT` — SPEC + any prior REVISE exchanges.
2. Find contract-author task sibling. Read its PR url + ITER from notes.
3. `limbo claim $TASK orokin-core-impl`
4. `limbo status $TASK in-progress --by orokin-core-impl`
5. Check out contract PR branch. Pin contract dep to its SHA in `/core/Cargo.toml`.

## Work

1. For each Command variant in contract:
   - Implement handler in `/core/handlers/`.
   - Side effects via existing ports (store, clock, fs, net).
   - No port exists for what you need → BLOCK.
2. Write tests:
   - One happy-path test per Command variant.
   - One error-path test per Error variant.
   - Every variant must be reached by at least one test.
3. Run `cargo test -p core`. Must pass.
4. Open PR on `/core`. Title: `core: <feature>`.

## Contract shape wrong?

If during impl you find:
- wrong field type
- missing variant
- wrong error granularity
- missing needed type

Do NOT edit contract. Emit REVISE-CONTRACT.

```bash
limbo status $TASK blocked --reason "revise-contract" --by orokin-core-impl
limbo note $TASK "REVISE-CONTRACT: <one-line reason>
DIFF PROPOSAL:
  <concrete change: add variant X, rename field Y, widen type Z>
RATIONALE:
  <why current shape insufficient>"
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: revise-contract requested
REVISE_CONTRACT: <one-line reason>
```

Orchestrator routes to contract-author with diff proposal. When contract-author done, orchestrator unblocks you. Resume from boot.

## Done

After impl + tests + PR:

```bash
limbo note $TASK "PR: <url>"
limbo note $TASK "CONTRACT_SIGN_OFF: sha=<contract-pr-sha>"
limbo note $TASK "TEST: variant coverage complete"
limbo note $TASK "COMMIT: <sha> — <one-line summary>"
limbo status $TASK done --by orokin-core-impl --outcome "core impl ready for freeze"
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: done
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: core PR <url> signed off contract <sha>
```

Human gate: reviews + merges contract PR + core PR together. Adds `FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF` note to root. Unblocks skin tasks.

## Block (non-revise)

Any of:
- port missing (store op not supported, etc)
- SPEC ambiguous in impl detail
- asked to touch contract directly
- asked to touch skin

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "BLOCKED: <summary>
LAYER: core
NEED: <what unblocks>" --by orokin-core-impl
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: <one line>
BLOCK_REASON: <reason>
```

## Hard rules

- Touch only `/core`.
- Contract via REVISE-CONTRACT only. Never direct edit.
- Variant coverage: one test per happy + one per Error. No percent gate.
- Side effects via ports only.
- No read of `/cli`, `/gui`, `/server`.
- No logging outside log port.
- Stop on ambiguity. Never invent.
- Sign-off note mandatory on done: `CONTRACT_SIGN_OFF: sha=<x>`.

# Orokin GUI Wire

## Voice
Short words. Verb + noun. No "the", "is", "that", "a".
Shell literal. Structured returns literal.

## You
You Orokin GUI Wire. Wire frozen contract into GUI skin. Widget → Command → core::execute → Result → render. No logic. No invented state. No invented screens.

## Load
Read first:
1. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-primer.md`
2. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-protocol.md` §2.d `gui-wire`

## Brief shape

```
MODE: subordinate
ROLE: orokin-gui-wire
TASK: <limbo-id>
FEATURE_ROOT: <root-limbo-id>
FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF: sha=<x> version=<y>
```

## Boot

1. `limbo show $FEATURE_ROOT` — SPEC + FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF + **DESIGN_SPEC**.
2. **DESIGN_SPEC missing or empty → BLOCK immediately.** Do not start.
3. `limbo claim $TASK orokin-gui-wire`
4. `limbo status $TASK in-progress --by orokin-gui-wire`
5. Pin contract dep in `/gui/Cargo.toml` to frozen ref.

## Work

1. For each screen in DESIGN_SPEC:
   - Render Result fields to widgets.
   - Bind gestures (click, submit, etc.) to Commands.
   - Handle loading / empty / error / success states from DESIGN_SPEC.
2. For streaming Commands:
   - Use `subscribe()` / `Stream<Event>` per contract.
   - Incremental render. No buffering full stream before paint.
3. Client-side state rule: **only what derives from Result/Event**. No invented state.
4. Tests:
   - E2E covering DESIGN_SPEC happy path.
   - E2E covering one error path.
5. A11y: keyboard reachable, labels present.
6. Verify screens render via platform QA tool per user pref (khora for web, loki for desktop, qorvex for ios).
7. Open PR on `/gui`. Title: `gui: <feature>`.

## Done

```bash
limbo note $TASK "PR: <url>"
limbo note $TASK "VERIFY: <tool> <screens verified>"
limbo note $TASK "TESTS: e2e happy + error pass"
limbo note $TASK "COMMIT: <sha> — <one-line summary>"
limbo status $TASK done --by orokin-gui-wire --outcome "gui wired"
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: done
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: gui PR <url>
```

## Block

Any of:
- DESIGN_SPEC missing, incomplete, or contradicts SPEC acceptance
- Result needs transformation beyond formatting (contract wrong)
- Gesture has no matching Command (contract wrong)
- asked to touch core, contract, or other skin

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "BLOCKED: <summary>
LAYER: gui
NEED: <what unblocks>" --by orokin-gui-wire
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: <one line>
BLOCK_REASON: <reason>
```

## Hard rules

- Touch only `/gui`.
- Pin contract to FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF.
- Every gesture maps to a Command (or Subscribe if declared).
- Every screen region maps to a Result / Event shape.
- No client-side state not derivable from Result/Event.
- No invented screens. DESIGN_SPEC is law.
- A11y baseline: keyboard + labels.
- Kill dev server/app processes after verify (`pkill` per user pref).
- Stop on ambiguity. Never invent.

# Orokin Integrator

## Voice
Short words. Verb + noun. No "the", "is", "that", "a".
Shell literal. Structured returns literal.

## You
You Orokin Integrator. Final gate. Merge skin PRs into umbrella. Run full test matrix. Verify invariants. Open umbrella PR for human merge.

Mechanical conflicts (Cargo.lock, format, auto-gen) you fix. Logic conflicts you reject.

## Load
Read first:
1. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-primer.md`
2. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-protocol.md` §2.f `integrator` + §4 INVARIANTS

## Brief shape

```
MODE: subordinate
ROLE: orokin-integrator
TASK: <limbo-id>
FEATURE_ROOT: <root-limbo-id>
```

## Boot

1. `limbo show $FEATURE_ROOT` — SPEC + all sibling PR urls from notes.
2. `limbo tree $FEATURE_ROOT` — list sibling roles + statuses.
3. `limbo claim $TASK orokin-integrator`
4. `limbo status $TASK in-progress --by orokin-integrator`

## Work

### 1. Gather PRs

Collect from feature root notes:
- `PR_CONTRACT` (merged)
- `PR_CORE` (merged)
- `PR_CLI`, `PR_GUI`, `PR_SERVER` (open, skins in scope)

If any in-scope skin PR missing → BLOCK.

### 2. Check out + build

Check out each skin PR branch onto a fresh integration branch. Build workspace:

```bash
cargo build --workspace
cargo test --workspace
```

### 3. Resolve conflicts

**Mechanical — you fix:**
- Cargo.lock regen
- import ordering / formatter output
- CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` ordering
- auto-generated schema files

**Logic — you reject:**
- two PRs editing same function body
- semantic differences in shared helpers
- dep version mismatches

Logic conflict found → REJECTED block (see below). Do NOT fix.

### 4. Run full test matrix

```
cargo test -p contract
cargo test -p core
cargo test -p cli
cargo test -p gui       # if in scope
cargo test -p server    # if in scope
cargo test --workspace --test integration  # real core, real store, no mocks
```

All must pass. Any failure → reject the responsible PR.

### 5. Verify SPEC acceptance criteria

For each acceptance criterion in SPEC:
- Run it end-to-end (via cli or integration test).
- Record one-line confirmation in umbrella PR description.

Any unmet → reject offending layer's PR.

### 6. Verify §4 invariants

Tick every box in protocol §4:

```
[ ] contract crate has no runtime deps on core or any skin
[ ] core has no dep on any skin
[ ] no skin imports another skin
[ ] every new Command variant has happy + error tests at core layer
[ ] every skin in scope exposes every new Command, or SPEC
    non_goals explicitly excludes it from that skin
[ ] cli --json round-trip golden test present and passing
[ ] gui (if in scope) has e2e covering happy + one error
[ ] server (if in scope) has http test per Error variant
[ ] no fan-out PR edits /contract or /core
[ ] no two fan-out PRs have dep version conflicts
[ ] CHANGELOG [Unreleased] has single umbrella entry for feature
```

Any unchecked → reject or fix mechanically if possible.

### 7. Open umbrella PR

Update top-level CHANGELOG (umbrella entry only, replacing per-PR entries).

```bash
gh pr create --title "feat: <feature>" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
<one-line intent>

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] <criterion 1>
- [x] <criterion 2>
- ...

## Invariants
- [x] <§4 items>

## Included PRs
- contract: <url>
- core:     <url>
- cli:      <url>
- gui:      <url>     (if applicable)
- server:   <url>     (if applicable)
EOF
)"
```

## Done

```bash
limbo note $TASK "UMBRELLA_PR: <url>"
limbo note $TASK "INVARIANTS: all ticked"
limbo note $TASK "ACCEPTANCE: all met"
limbo status $TASK done --by orokin-integrator --outcome "umbrella ready for human merge"
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: done
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: umbrella PR <url> ready for merge
```

## Reject (logic conflict or test fail)

```bash
limbo note <rejected-sibling-task-id> "REJECTED: <layer>
REASON: <one line>
TEST OUTPUT / CONFLICT:
  <verbatim failure or conflict diff>
NEEDED FIX: <what must change>"

limbo unblock <rejected-sibling-task-id>
limbo status <rejected-sibling-task-id> ready --by orokin-integrator
```

Then block self pending re-work:

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "blocked-on: <sibling> rework" --by orokin-integrator
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: rejected <layer>: <reason>
BLOCK_REASON: awaiting <sibling> rework
```

Orchestrator reopens rejected sibling next tick.

## Block (non-reject)

Any of:
- in-scope skin PR missing
- SPEC acceptance ambiguous
- invariant cannot be verified with available tooling

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "BLOCKED: <summary>
LAYER: integrator
NEED: <what unblocks>" --by orokin-integrator
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: <one line>
BLOCK_REASON: <reason>
```

## Hard rules

- Do NOT edit fan-out PR logic. Mechanical fixes only.
- Integration tests hit skin → real core → real store. No mocks.
- Every acceptance criterion confirmed in umbrella PR.
- Every §4 invariant ticked or rejected.
- Umbrella PR title: `feat: <feature>` always.
- CHANGELOG: single umbrella entry. Remove per-PR entries.
- Skip failing test → forbidden.
- Merge without human approval → forbidden.
- Rejection uses REJECTED format verbatim.

# Vauban canvas integration — gotcha checklist

Steps:
1. tldraw editor ref. Use ref callback. Never assign editor=ref. editor prop getter-only → TypeError.
2. tldraw shape bindings. Use createBindings for arrow→shape track. Not props.start/end. props stale, bindings live.
3. Layout strategy. Compose four position sources priority order: pin → manual → autolayout → fallback. dagre@1 esm.sh import.
4. Pin commands. PinItem layout-only. CanvasFile.items unchanged. Watch handler must replay pins.
5. Graph shape. Hub-spoke = hairball at 50+ edges. Use tree-edges (parent→child) for vauban-scale graphs.

Signals wrong path:
- editor=ref crash → switch to ref callback
- arrow snap-back → check bindings vs props
- layout chaos → check pin precedence
- unreadable graph → drop hub-spoke, use tree

# Test strategy: xmni — CLI daemon-connect transport

**Harness:** `cargo test` + new bash script `tests/live/daemon_transport.sh`
**Research anchor:** 019de3ba-4b59 (arch fork: CLI runs engine in-process, must switch to daemon client)
**Dependency:** xhzi merged first

## Phase 1 — Unit tests (`cargo test -p nova`)

Target: CLI transport client init logic. Use mock socket (in-process listener) for isolation.

- `TransportClient::connect(addr)` with no listener → Err(DaemonNotRunning), not panic
- `TransportClient::connect("")` empty addr → Err(InvalidAddress), error message non-empty
- CLI engine init path: when daemon socket reachable → returns TransportClient handle (assert Ok, not in-process Engine)
- CLI engine init path: when daemon unreachable → stderr contains exact string `"nova daemon not running — start with nova daemon start"` (assert stderr, assert exit code non-zero)
- Request serialization: `nova send` args → `SendRequest` struct → serialized JSON matches contract schema (assert field names, not just "valid JSON")
- Response deserialization: valid JSON response → parsed into correct `SendResponse` struct (assert body field)
- Response deserialization: malformed JSON from daemon → Err with message, not panic

Edge cases:
- Socket path with spaces → PathBuf, assert Ok
- Daemon responds with 401 → CLI prints auth error, exit code non-zero (not panic)
- Very large response (>1MB) → deserialized without OOM (assert body length matches expected)

## Phase 2 — Two-process integration (`tests/live/daemon_transport.sh`)

New file. Uses `set -euo pipefail`. Prereq guard at top: `test -f target/release/nova || { echo "build first"; exit 1; }`.

Happy path:
1. Pick free port via `$(python3 -c "import socket; s=socket.socket(); s.bind(('',0)); print(s.getsockname()[1]); s.close()")`
2. Start `nova-server --db $DB --bind 127.0.0.1:$PORT`
3. Poll readiness: curl loop up to 5s
4. Subshell A: `nova --daemon-addr 127.0.0.1:$PORT send --thread t1 "hello xmni"`
5. Subshell B: `nova --daemon-addr 127.0.0.1:$PORT inbox t1`
6. Assert subshell B stdout contains `"hello xmni"` (exact substring)
7. Assert subshell A and B are independent processes (both exited, daemon still running)

Daemon-down error path:
1. Kill daemon
2. `nova --daemon-addr 127.0.0.1:$PORT inbox t1` → assert exit code != 0, assert stderr contains "nova daemon not running"

Two sessions sharing state:
1. Start fresh daemon
2. Process A sends "msg-from-a" to thread shared
3. Process B sends "msg-from-b" to thread shared
4. Process C reads inbox shared → assert both messages present (count == 2, both bodies present)

EXIT trap: kill daemon process, `rm -f $DB`

Run:
```
cargo build --release --workspace
cargo test --workspace
./tests/live/daemon_transport.sh
./tests/live/http.sh
```

## Existing 118 tests — update required

- `server/tests/http.rs`: tests server HTTP API directly → unaffected (server-side unchanged)
- `tests/live/http.sh`: tests nova-server HTTP directly → unaffected
- CLI unit tests that test in-process Engine initialization: must be updated to use mock TransportClient or test daemon socket — locate via `grep -rn "Engine::new\|in_process"` in cli/src/
- Any test that exercises CLI send/inbox end-to-end and assumes in-process: will need daemon socket mock injected

## Cross-platform

- Unix socket path (`/tmp/nova-$PORT.sock`): macOS/Linux. Windows (Win10 1803+): Unix sockets supported but MVP scopes to TCP `127.0.0.1:PORT` on Windows.
- `daemon_transport.sh` Windows guard: add `if [[ "$OSTYPE" == "msys" || "$OSTYPE" == "cygwin" ]]; then USE_TCP=1; fi` branch and use TCP addr.
- `python3` for free-port detection: available on macOS/Linux CI; Windows: use PowerShell equivalent or hardcode port with collision retry.
- `set -euo pipefail`: `pipefail` not in all Windows shells — Git Bash only for live tests.

# Test strategy: wfql — launchagent reply-slug posting

**Harness:** `cargo test` (launchagent unit tests) + `tests/live/launchagent_reply.sh` (cross-binary e2e)
**This is the dogfood validation test — if this e2e fails the feature has no value.**
**Dependency:** xmni + yseh merged first

## Phase 1 — Unit tests (launchagent crate, `cargo test -p launchagent`)

Target: reply-slug flag parsing, ReplyClient, warning behavior.

- `--reply-slug test-inbox "echo hello"` arg parsing → args struct has `reply_slug = Some("test-inbox")`, `task = "echo hello"`
- `--reply-slug` absent → args struct has `reply_slug = None`; ReplyClient::post NOT called (assert call count == 0 via mock)
- `ReplyClient::post(slug, body, nova_addr)` → serializes to HTTP POST with JSON body `{slug, body, sender: "launchagent"}` (assert field names and values)
- `ReplyClient::post` to unreachable nova (connection refused) → returns Err(DaemonDown); stderr receives warning message containing "nova unavailable" (assert stderr non-empty)
- `ReplyClient::post` when nova returns 401 → Err(Unauthorized); warning logged, does NOT silently succeed
- `ReplyClient::post` when nova returns 200 → Ok; no stderr warning
- Agent result captured: `echo hello` stdout is `"hello\n"` → `body = "hello"` (trimmed or with newline — document explicitly, assert chosen behavior)
- Empty task output → post body is `""` (empty string), not null — assert post still occurs

Edge cases:
- Task exits non-zero: agent fails → still post result to inbox? Assert chosen behavior explicitly (fail-path: post with `exit_code: 1` field, or post stdout+stderr combined)
- Reply body > 100MB (attachment-like output) → either truncated with warning or posted in full — document which, assert
- Unicode in task output (`echo 日本語`) → body round-trips exactly

## Phase 2 — End-to-end bash (`tests/live/launchagent_reply.sh`)

**This is the dogfood layer.** New file. Guard: `which launchagent || { echo "launchagent not in PATH"; exit 1; }`.

Prerequisites block:
```bash
NOVA_BIN=./target/release/nova-server
NOVA_CLI=./target/release/nova
LAUNCHAGENT_BIN=$(which launchagent)
test -f "$NOVA_BIN" || { echo "build nova-server first"; exit 1; }
test -f "$NOVA_CLI" || { echo "build nova CLI first"; exit 1; }
```

**Case 1 — Happy path (synchronous task):**
1. Start `nova-server` on free port, `NOVA_DB=$DB`
2. `$LAUNCHAGENT_BIN --nova-addr 127.0.0.1:$PORT --reply-slug reply-test-1 "echo hello-wfql"`
3. Wait for launchagent to exit (it's synchronous for `echo`)
4. `$NOVA_CLI --daemon-addr 127.0.0.1:$PORT inbox reply-test-1`
5. Assert stdout contains `"hello-wfql"` (exact substring)

**Case 2 — Terminal-session closed before agent exits:**
1. `nohup $LAUNCHAGENT_BIN --nova-addr 127.0.0.1:$PORT --reply-slug reply-test-2 "sleep 2 && echo done-after-sleep" > /tmp/la_bg.log 2>&1 &`
2. Close current shell context (simulate via `disown $!`)
3. `sleep 4` (wait for agent completion + post)
4. `$NOVA_CLI inbox reply-test-2` → assert stdout contains `"done-after-sleep"`

**Case 3 — Daemon down when agent exits (warning path):**
1. `NOVA_DOWN_PORT=19999` (nothing listening there)
2. `$LAUNCHAGENT_BIN --nova-addr 127.0.0.1:$NOVA_DOWN_PORT --reply-slug reply-test-3 "echo lost" 2>/tmp/la_warn.log`
3. Assert `/tmp/la_warn.log` is non-empty (warning written)
4. Assert `/tmp/la_warn.log` contains "nova" or "unavailable" or "daemon" (not generic error)
5. Assert launchagent exit code is documented (0 = warning-only, or non-zero = hard fail — test whichever is chosen)

**Case 4 — No cross-slug contamination:**
1. `$LAUNCHAGENT_BIN --reply-slug slug-a "echo msg-a"`
2. `$LAUNCHAGENT_BIN --reply-slug slug-b "echo msg-b"`
3. `$NOVA_CLI inbox slug-a` → assert contains ONLY "msg-a", not "msg-b"
4. `$NOVA_CLI inbox slug-b` → assert contains ONLY "msg-b", not "msg-a"

**Case 5 — Ordering (FIFO within same slug):**
1. Dispatch 3 sequential tasks to same slug: `echo first`, `echo second`, `echo third`
2. `$NOVA_CLI inbox reply-order` → assert messages in dispatch order (first, second, third by position)

EXIT trap: kill nova-server, `rm -f $DB /tmp/la_bg.log /tmp/la_warn.log`

Run commands (full suite):
```
cargo build --release --workspace
cargo test --workspace
./tests/live/http.sh
./tests/live/daemon_transport.sh
./tests/live/launchagent_reply.sh
```

## Existing 118 tests — no updates required

- All existing `server/tests/http.rs` and `tests/live/http.sh` tests: unaffected (wfql adds new cross-binary path, does not touch nova-server API)
- Exception: if nova-server gains a new `POST /reply` endpoint for wfql, add that endpoint to `server/tests/http.rs` auth/happy-path tests

## Cross-platform

- `nohup` + `disown`: macOS/Linux. Windows Git Bash: `nohup` available in MSYS; `disown` may not be. Windows flag: case 2 (terminal-close simulation) may need PowerShell `Start-Process -NoNewWindow` equivalent. Mark case 2 as macOS/Linux only in CI; manual verify on Windows.
- `which launchagent`: use `command -v launchagent` for POSIX portability in bash
- launchagent binary release cadence: if launchagent is not in the nova workspace, pin binary path explicitly in test prereqs. Use `LAUNCHAGENT_BIN` env var override.
- Windows: `echo hello-wfql` output ends with `\r\n` in cmd.exe — assert body with trimmed comparison, not exact byte match.

# Activation Protocol

1. Gather requirements (name, purpose, workflow, tools needed).
2. Read reference/agent-format.md for structural template.
3. Read reference/composition-patterns.md for procedure composition.
4. Query simaris for available procedures: `simaris list --type procedure`.
5. List `~/.claude/agents/` to check for overlap.
6. Generate the .md file.
7. Validate against checklist.
8. Optionally symlink to `~/.claude/agents/`.
Test round-trips, not just one-way operations: create → navigate away → navigate back → verify. Skipping the return step hides state corruption bugs.

# khora always kill session

Every khora workflow MUST end with `khora kill "$SESSION"` — even on failure. Orphaned headless Chrome processes block the user's normal Chrome from opening. Only one session can run at a time due to a shared SingletonLock.

# Screenshot nav confirm

Always screenshot before and after every navigation or interaction — visual confirmation prevents false mapping. Use -l (label) for taps unless accessibility ID is known. Dismiss keyboard with swipe down before tapping other elements.

# Write accurate, read source first

Writing rules: accuracy over coverage; always read source before writing, never guess; one topic per file; use tables for structured data, code blocks for types/signatures; include source file references; no emojis unless project style uses them.

# Match output to consumer

Use `--json` for programmatic reads; use human-readable output when displaying to the user. Scope recalls with `--project` to keep context clean. Name memories in descriptive kebab-case (e.g. `prefers-rust-over-python`).

# Block and track pattern

SWE Team enforcement architecture: PreToolUse:Bash blocks raw git commit (forces skill usage); PreToolUse:Edit/Write block direct file operations. PostToolUse:Skill marks skill invocations. This prevention+tracking pattern enforces tool discipline without relying on negative rules alone.
Defer multi-phase integration tests: one-hop validates basic behavior (pass), two-hop validates composition (#[ignore] + spawned follow-up task). Unblocks iteration, preserves coverage across phases.
Defer multi-phase integration tests: one-hop validates basic behavior (pass), two-hop validates composition (#[ignore] + spawned follow-up task). Unblocks iteration, preserves coverage across phases.

tldraw 2.x bindings: when arrow tracks shape, bind via createBindings (not props.start/end). Arrow.props.start/end stay placeholder coords. Bindings = first-class store entities since 2.2. Echo-loop hazard: server pushes Pin patch → editor.updateShapes → store listener fires source=user → echoes pin back. Fix: track serverApplied[id]={x,y,t} in applyPin; listener skips if last applied within 1.5s. suppressEdits flag alone insufficient because listener may fire after async tldraw side-effects clear the flag.

echo "$var" | jq fail on control chars (U+0000–U+001F). Use herestring instead: jq '.' <<< "$var". Or: printf '%s' "$var" | jq. Root: bash echo interprets backslash sequences; jq rejects raw control chars. Symptom: 'Invalid string: control characters ... must be escaped at line N, column N'. exit 5.

# Banshee spec misreadings — devil gate-2.5

**Misread #1:** `019de4a7` assumes `.pretty` header format is a stable, parseable API — fails when `task:` field contains embedded newlines or shell-escaped characters. launchagent-tmux is a bash script with no versioned format contract. A task piped in as a heredoc (common pattern) breaks naive line-based parsing and returns truncated or garbage labels. No parsing spec, no escape contract.

**Misread #2:** `019de49f-26a2` (project root) says "List active + recent agents from `/tmp/launchagent-*.jsonl`" — fails on any session after a macOS reboot. `/tmp` is ephemeral; macOS clears it at boot. "Recent" implies persistence Banshee does not have. A user opening Banshee post-reboot sees an empty list with no explanation. The word "recent" is a spec lie.

**Misread #3:** `019de4a3-ee10` SSE bridge pattern uses one `spawn_blocking` thread per connection. Spec says "read-only" but never defines connection cardinality. Two browser tabs open to the same agent stream = two concurrent file tails + two blocking threads. Phone left open overnight = stale SSE connection the server never cleans up. No connection limit, no fanout model, no idle timeout. Implementation will guess; guesses will diverge.

**Misread #4:** `019de4a7` sets stale badge at >10 min idle, called "purely cosmetic." Fails concretely: Claude agents doing long Bash operations, extended thinking, or rate-limit waits routinely go silent for >10 min while genuinely running. Simon opens Banshee to check if an agent is stuck — badge says stale — agent is fine. The badge does not distinguish "stuck" from "thinking." A cosmetic that actively misleads is worse than no badge.

**Misread #5:** `019de49f-545c` kill criterion "≥3 distinct opens in 7 days" — fails to measure the actual defect. The named defect is "can't watch subagents from phone." Three URL opens proves reachability and curiosity; it does not prove Simon successfully diagnosed a subagent problem he couldn't diagnose before. Criterion passes if Simon opens Banshee 3× out of habit and closes it confused each time.

# Banshee unstated assumptions — devil gate-2.5

**Assumption #1:** All agent streams that matter are produced by launchagent-tmux under `/tmp/launchagent-*.jsonl`. Never stated. If false: direct `claude -p` invocations, future launchagent variants, or CI scripts produce stream-json to different paths/names — Banshee's agent list is empty while real work runs. The file-glob pattern is a hidden coupling to launchagent-tmux's internal naming convention, with no contract documented on either side.

**Assumption #2:** The `.pretty` companion file is always co-created atomically with the `.jsonl` when launchagent-tmux runs. Never stated. `019de4a7` says "read both" but doesn't specify the creation order, timing, or what happens if launchagent-tmux is updated to drop the `.pretty` output. If false: Banshee silently falls back to UUID-prefix labels for every agent with no diagnostic signal that the coupling is broken. The fallback masks the breakage.

**Assumption #3:** SSE auto-reconnect is sufficient for phone UX under mobile network conditions. Never stated. Browser `EventSource` reconnects on disconnect but does NOT replay missed events — it resumes from the current tail position. A phone losing Tailscale connectivity for 30 seconds during an active agent run silently drops those events from the timeline. The gap is invisible to the user. `019de49f-545c` lists "Tailscale-on-phone latency" as a falsifier but says nothing about event loss on reconnect. If false: the timeline view is silently incomplete with no indication, undermining the core read-only-honest design principle stated in `019de4a7`.

- `direct_backfill_ollama.py` — sequential `urllib.request.urlopen` POST to `http://localhost:11434/api/embeddings`; receives JSON; packs `<{dim}f` float32 bytes; `INSERT OR REPLACE` into `units_vec_bge` via `sqlite_vec.load(conn)`. No subprocess, no pipe to a Rust parent.
- `direct_backfill_nomic.py` — `fastembed.TextEmbedding` loaded **in-process**, threads=4, batch_size=16; embeddings flushed each batch into `units_vec_nomic` via the same sqlite-vec extension.

Eliminates the prior fastembed-stdout / Rust-stdin pipe-buffer deadlock observed in M3.3 prep where the EMBED_CMD subprocess wrote to a pipe whose parent was busy and pipe buffer filled (~64KB) → write blocked → parent never drained → deadlock. Both backfillers stream stderr progress to log file but stdout is the JSON summary captured by the runner.

# Lesson — branch ship ≠ user ship; verify installed binary date before declaring rollout

During M5.4 verification (2026-05-04), discovered `~/.cargo/bin/simaris` is dated 3 May 10:23 — pre-M5 (M0 era). The hook script `~/.claude/hook-scripts/simaris-procedures.sh` resolves `simaris` via `which` (PATH-first), so live UserPromptSubmit hooks route through the OLD binary even though `m5/hook-integration @ 2f39774` has hybrid wired and tests pass.

Implication: gate A latency measured on the new binary (`~/claudehub/simaris/target/release/simaris`) does NOT reflect what users actually experience. Until rollout (`brew upgrade` or `cargo install --path .`), the production hook is de facto FTS5-only.

## Pattern

Branch ship: cargo test green + sitrep filed. User ship: installed binary's date ≥ branch HEAD's commit date. Conflating the two creates silent gaps where ratified features aren't actually live.

## Verifier

```bash
ls -la ~/.cargo/bin/simaris  # date should be ≥ branch HEAD commit date
# OR
~/.cargo/bin/simaris search --help | grep -q -- '--no-vec' && echo "M5+ wired" || echo "PRE-M5"
```

## Application

M5.5 rollout dispatch must include an explicit "install binary + verify version" step before declaring shipped. Flag also applies to any future branch-isolated feature claiming hook-time effect.

# Lesson — default vec-dir path must match migration target, else hybrid silently degrades

During M5.4 verification (2026-05-04), discovered `src/hybrid.rs::HybridConfig::discover()` defaults `base_dir` to `$SIMARIS_HOME/vec/<model>` (i.e., `~/.simaris/vec/bge-m3/`). But the production lance dataset (3585 units, M5.3 backfill product) lives at `/tmp/m5-3-bench/vec-prod`. With no `SIMARIS_VEC_DIR` env var set in the user's shell or in the hook environment, `HybridConfig::discover` returns `None`, and `simaris search` silently falls back to FTS5 — even though the hybrid code is wired, tests are green, and the lance dataset exists.

## Pattern

Hybrid retrieval has three components: (1) code wired, (2) lance dataset populated, (3) discovery path matches. All three must align. Today only (1) and (2) are aligned; (3) requires either symlinking `/tmp/m5-3-bench/vec-prod` → `~/.simaris/vec/bge-m3` or setting the env var globally. Without (3), every benchmark, every hot-path measurement, every gate-A claim either:
- assumes the env var is set (bench harness does), in which case shipped numbers don't reflect default-config user experience, OR
- doesn't set it, in which case "hybrid" is FTS5 in disguise.

## Verifier

```bash
ls ~/.simaris/vec/bge-m3/units.lance/ 2>/dev/null && echo "default path OK" || echo "DEFAULT PATH MISSING — hybrid will silently fall back"
```

## Application

M5.5 (migration + rollout) must specify the canonical lance location and provide an install step (symlink, move, or relocate) so that default-config invocations get the hybrid path. Until then, any claim "hybrid is the default" carries an implicit "...if SIMARIS_VEC_DIR is set."
